verses.text: Hezekiah comes to the throne after wicked Ahaz and does right in the eyes of the LORD, as David had. A godly son rises from an ungodly father. Grace is not bound by heredity; God can raise a reformer out of a ruined house.

Hezekiah comes to the throne after wicked Ahaz and does right in the eyes of the LORD, as David had. A godly son rises from an ungodly father. Grace is not bound by heredity; God can raise a reformer out of a ruined house.

In the first month of his reign Hezekiah opens the temple doors his father shut and calls the Levites: 'sanctify yourselves... carry forth the filthiness out of the holy place.' He begins at once and begins with God's house. True reform starts by cleansing the place of worship.

In the first month of his reign Hezekiah opens the temple doors his father shut and calls the Levites: 'sanctify yourselves... carry forth the filthiness out of the holy place.' He begins at once and begins with God's house. True reform starts by cleansing the place of worship.

In the first month of his reign Hezekiah opens the temple doors his father shut and calls the Levites: 'sanctify yourselves... carry forth the filthiness out of the holy place.' He begins at once and begins with God's house. True reform starts by cleansing the place of worship.

Hezekiah confesses that the fathers had trespassed, turned their backs on the LORD, and shut the doors, so wrath fell on Judah. He owns the nation's sin honestly. Repentance does not excuse the past but names it plainly as the cause of the trouble.

Hezekiah confesses that the fathers had trespassed, turned their backs on the LORD, and shut the doors, so wrath fell on Judah. He owns the nation's sin honestly. Repentance does not excuse the past but names it plainly as the cause of the trouble.

Hezekiah confesses that the fathers had trespassed, turned their backs on the LORD, and shut the doors, so wrath fell on Judah. He owns the nation's sin honestly. Repentance does not excuse the past but names it plainly as the cause of the trouble.

'Now it is in mine heart to make a covenant with the LORD,' he says, urging, 'be not now negligent.' He calls for prompt, wholehearted return. When God stirs a desire to set things right, delay is the enemy; the time to act is now.

'Now it is in mine heart to make a covenant with the LORD,' he says, urging, 'be not now negligent.' He calls for prompt, wholehearted return. When God stirs a desire to set things right, delay is the enemy; the time to act is now.

The Levites rise, sanctify themselves, and gather to cleanse the house as the king commanded by the words of the LORD. They answer the call without delay. A leader's zeal, joined to a willing people, sets the work of God swiftly in motion.

The Levites rise, sanctify themselves, and gather to cleanse the house as the king commanded by the words of the LORD. They answer the call without delay. A leader's zeal, joined to a willing people, sets the work of God swiftly in motion.

The Levites rise, sanctify themselves, and gather to cleanse the house as the king commanded by the words of the LORD. They answer the call without delay. A leader's zeal, joined to a willing people, sets the work of God swiftly in motion.

The Levites rise, sanctify themselves, and gather to cleanse the house as the king commanded by the words of the LORD. They answer the call without delay. A leader's zeal, joined to a willing people, sets the work of God swiftly in motion.

They cleanse the temple in eight days and the porch in eight more, carrying out all the uncleanness. The neglected house is thoroughly purged. Restoring what was let go takes patient, unglamorous labour, but it clears the way for worship to return.

They cleanse the temple in eight days and the porch in eight more, carrying out all the uncleanness. The neglected house is thoroughly purged. Restoring what was let go takes patient, unglamorous labour, but it clears the way for worship to return.

They report to the king that all is cleansed and the vessels Ahaz had cast aside are restored and ready. The house is fit for service again. What sin discards, repentance recovers, and the instruments of worship are set in their place once more.

They report to the king that all is cleansed and the vessels Ahaz had cast aside are restored and ready. The house is fit for service again. What sin discards, repentance recovers, and the instruments of worship are set in their place once more.

Hezekiah offers sin offerings for the kingdom, the sanctuary, and Judah; the blood is sprinkled to make atonement for all Israel. Cleansing must be sealed by sacrifice. No reform is complete until sin is covered by the blood that God has appointed.

Hezekiah offers sin offerings for the kingdom, the sanctuary, and Judah; the blood is sprinkled to make atonement for all Israel. Cleansing must be sealed by sacrifice. No reform is complete until sin is covered by the blood that God has appointed.

He sets the Levites with cymbals, harps, and trumpets as David, Gad, and Nathan ordained, 'for so was the commandment of the LORD by his prophets.' Worship follows God's pattern, not man's taste. Acceptable praise is offered the way the LORD has prescribed.

He sets the Levites with cymbals, harps, and trumpets as David, Gad, and Nathan ordained, 'for so was the commandment of the LORD by his prophets.' Worship follows God's pattern, not man's taste. Acceptable praise is offered the way the LORD has prescribed.

As the burnt offering begins, the song of the LORD and the trumpets sound, and all worship and bow with gladness, singing praises. Sacrifice and song rise together. When God is honoured rightly, joy fills His house and bursts forth in praise.

As the burnt offering begins, the song of the LORD and the trumpets sound, and all worship and bow with gladness, singing praises. Sacrifice and song rise together. When God is honoured rightly, joy fills His house and bursts forth in praise.

As the burnt offering begins, the song of the LORD and the trumpets sound, and all worship and bow with gladness, singing praises. Sacrifice and song rise together. When God is honoured rightly, joy fills His house and bursts forth in praise.

Hezekiah declares, 'ye have consecrated yourselves unto the LORD; come near,' and the people bring sacrifices and thank offerings in abundance. Consecration opens the way to draw near. Hearts set apart for God respond freely and generously in worship.

Hezekiah declares, 'ye have consecrated yourselves unto the LORD; come near,' and the people bring sacrifices and thank offerings in abundance. Consecration opens the way to draw near. Hearts set apart for God respond freely and generously in worship.

Hezekiah declares, 'ye have consecrated yourselves unto the LORD; come near,' and the people bring sacrifices and thank offerings in abundance. Consecration opens the way to draw near. Hearts set apart for God respond freely and generously in worship.

There are too few priests to flay the offerings, so the Levites help, and Hezekiah and the people rejoice that God had prepared the people, 'for the thing was done suddenly.' Revival came swiftly because God moved hearts. When He prepares a people, His work outruns men's expectation.
